title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers in Brookline authorize strike as contract talks with management remain stalled.
Union concerns focus on job protections and the impact of self-service kiosks on employment.
Management asserts commitment to fair negotiations, with no immediate plans for a strike announced.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts outfielder Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 MLB season.
FanGraphs projects no Red Sox player will hit 20 or more home runs this season, with Abreu expected to hit 18.
Abreu hit 22 home runs in 2025 and showcased power in the 2026 World Baseball Classic, hitting two home runs for Venezuela.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots projected to draft offensive tackle Max Iheanachor from Arizona State with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Iheanachor, a 6-foot-6, 321-pound player, was a full-time starter and earned Second Team All-Big 12 honors in 2025.
NFL experts believe Iheanachor has potential to develop into a high-level starter despite needing more refinement.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials condemned racist online comments following South High's basketball game against Andover on March 6.
Social media comments included threatening to call ICE and racist remarks against South High's players, calling for community action.
NAACP and YWCA supported South High, criticizing ongoing racially and sexually discriminatory stereotypes against Black girls.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, from Rehoboth, was arrested for sexually exploiting four minors aged 12 to 16, officials announced.
Authorities found hundreds of illegal images and videos in a hidden folder during a border search of Mota's phone.
Mota admitted to paying minors for explicit content and faces up to 30 years in prison if convicted.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection reopened after a water main break flooded streets, affecting traffic and nearby businesses.
The Boston Water and Sewer Commission initially misidentified the broken pipe size as 36 inches; it was actually 12 inches.
Repairs, including pipe replacement and road restoration, were completed within 48 hours, restoring all traffic lanes.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, indicted on three new rape counts and 17 indecent assault charges, involving 11 patients in Framingham.
Todd's alleged assaults occurred between 2021 and 2023 at Charles River Medical Associates, where he was a rheumatologist.
Over 200 former patients joined a class action suit against Todd, who ceased practicing at Brigham and Women’s in 2023.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: To convert a RAM van for van life, add a deep cycle or LiFePO battery and voltage inverter.
John Paul advises using a pure sine wave converter for cleaner power to run electronics and appliances.
Rooftop solar panels with a voltage regulator can charge the auxiliary battery for a van conversion.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against Matthew Cunningham, former Brockton choral director, were dropped after the alleged victim declined to testify.
Cunningham was arrested in August 2025 on charges of trafficking and sexual conduct with an underage boy.
Brockton Public Schools placed Cunningham on leave and confirmed he is no longer employed there.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old were arrested for an armed robbery in Charlestown involving an elderly woman.
The suspects allegedly pointed a gun at the victim and stole her purse and credit cards before fleeing the scene.
Jordan faces charges including armed robbery while the minor faces similar charges; both were caught following a short police pursuit.
